new_classes.definition_en: A spirituous beverage produced by distilling fermented agricultural products (typically cereals or potatoes), characterised by its neutral organoleptic character and minimum alcoholic strength of 37.5% vol, classified under CN heading 2208 60.
Vodka presented in containers of a nominal volume not exceeding 2 litres, classified under CN subheading 2208 60 11.

A quality borne by a spirituous beverage indicating that it has a neutral taste and odour profile, without pronounced distinctive character from raw materials, as required for vodka under the HS explanatory notes to heading 2208.

A distillation process applied to fermented agricultural products (cereals, potatoes, or other agricultural raw materials) to produce a spirituous beverage with neutral organoleptic character, used in the production of vodka classified under CN 2208 60.

coverage_explanation: The legal text for 22086011 is minimal ("Wodka – Siehe die Erläuterungen zu Position 2208 des HS, dritter Absatz, Ziffer 5"), referring to the HS Explanatory Notes for heading 2208, paragraph 3, item 5. The key distinguishing criteria captured are: (1) vodka is a spirituous beverage (subclass of SpiritousBeverage); (2) minimum alcoholic strength 37.5% vol (MinAlcohol37_5VolQuality, already in TBox); (3) neutral organoleptic character quality (new NeutralOrganolepticCharacterQuality); (4) produced by distillation (VodkaDistillationProcess); (5) container size ≤ 2 L for this specific subheading 22086011 (SmallContainer restriction on VodkaInSmallContainer). Coverage is reduced from 1.0 because: the HS Explanatory Notes also specify permitted raw materials (agricultural origin — cereals, potatoes, molasses, etc.) and permitted treatments (charcoal filtration, additives up to small quantities), which are referenced by cross-reference only and cannot be fully formalised without the full HS note text. The sibling subheading 22086091/99 (> 2 L containers) is not modelled here (different CN code). The cross-reference nature of the legal note limits full coverage.
